axes.multi_agent: orthogonal flag — composes with any depth tier

mechanisms.note: recall-repetition acceptance-metric measurement tool (recall-triggering blueprint #1688 P1/#1689) -- a measurement/benchmarking harness for ENGRAM-development use, same class as tools/perf's replay-bench harness. Real file directly under repo-root tools/ (not mirrored under src/engram/tools/), so 'source' is set explicitly -- without it the manifest's default source_root-relative resolution (src/engram/tools/recall_repetition_analysis.py) would wrongly flag this as dangling.

replay-bench perf harness (blueprint #1667/#1668) -- a measurement/benchmarking harness for ENGRAM-development use, same class as the recall-measurement harness and other dev-only baselines. Real files directly under repo-root tools/perf/ (not symlinked from src/engram/), so 'source' is set explicitly -- without it the manifest's default source_root-relative resolution (src/engram/tools/perf) would wrongly flag this as dangling.

build_inputs.reason: Claude Code plugin manifest — copied into the bundle root by the build engine (tools/engine/build.py) as the bundle's own manifest; structural build input, not a depth-tier-gated mechanism
Hooks registration — the build engine writes a tier/identity-filtered copy into the bundle (hooks/hooks.json); structural build input, not itself a depth-tier-gated mechanism
MCP server registration source — the build engine transforms it into the bundle's .mcp.json (renamed at the plugin root); build input, not a depth-tier-gated mechanism
Per-platform profile sources (claude-code.json, codex.json) — baked by the build engine into the bundle's platform.json; build input, not a depth-tier-gated mechanism
The tier manifest itself — consumed by the build engine to select shippable mechanisms; build configuration input, not a depth-tier-gated mechanism
